steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the "main purpose" of the text. The passage describes Voss et al.'s CNN model and then states that its output aligned with prior direct-measurement studies — which Voss et al. cited as evidence that the model accurately represents biomechanical processes. The text is presenting that alignment as validation of the model.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "To present evidence from Voss et al.'s study in support of the efficacy of their CNN model.": The passage describes one piece of supporting evidence (alignment with prior measurements) that Voss et al. used to argue their model works. This matches the text's purpose exactly. ✓
  • "To provide an overview of how Voss et al. honed the accuracy of their CNN model.": The passage doesn't describe any refinement or tuning process — it presents a finished model and validation evidence, not a development process. ✗
  • "To compare results obtained by Voss et al. using their CNN model to prior results obtained from other researchers' models.": The comparison is between Voss et al.'s model output and direct measurements by other researchers, not between two models. ✗
  • "To account for Voss et al.'s reliance in their study on a CNN model in lieu of direct measurement.": The passage doesn't justify or explain why they used a CNN instead of direct measurement — it presents the model and evidence that it works. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
